_meta.note: calls-only trace (tool + args, {{RUN_DIR}}-templated); result envelopes deliberately NOT stored — replay re-executes and re-scores (design §7: storing them would leak KB-derived text into the repo).

args.script:
# Introspect Text DAT and GLSL TOP parameter names
import os

shader_path = r'{{RUN_DIR}}/assets/eval_fragment.glsl'
print("Shader file exists:", os.path.exists(shader_path))

# Check Text DAT params related to file sync
tmp_c = root.createCOMP('container', name='_tmp_inspect')
tmp_t = tmp_c.createDAT('text', name='t1')
file_pars = [p.name for p in tmp_t.pars() if 'file' in p.name.lower() or 'sync' in p.name.lower()]
print("Text DAT file/sync pars:", file_pars)

# Check GLSL TOP params related to pixel/shader DAT
tmp_g = tmp_c.createTOP('glsl', name='g1')
glsl_pars = [p.name for p in tmp_g.pars() if 'dat' in p.name.lower() or 'pixel' in p.name.lower() or 'shader' in p.name.lower() or 'vert' in p.name.lower()]
print("GLSL TOP shader DAT pars:", glsl_pars)

# Clean up temp container
tmp_c.destroy()

# Find a good parent to create in, and introspect param names
import os

shader_path = r'{{RUN_DIR}}/assets/eval_fragment.glsl'
print("Shader file exists:", os.path.exists(shader_path))
print("Shader content preview:")
with open(shader_path) as f:
    print(f.read()[:200])

# Check what's at root level
print("/nRoot children:", [c.name for c in root.children])

import os

shader_path = r'{{RUN_DIR}}/assets/eval_fragment.glsl'

# Save original content first
with open(shader_path, 'r') as f:
    original = f.read()
print("Original saved, length:", len(original))

# Write broken shader (invalid GLSL syntax)
broken = "this is not valid GLSL syntax @@@ INTENTIONAL_BREAK"
with open(shader_path, 'w') as f:
    f.write(broken)

print("Broken shader written.")
print("File now contains:", repr(broken))
